game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Cannon Shot!** The game presents a 2D physics puzzle where players manipulate the environment to guide projectiles. Players use a tap-and-drag interface to position and rotate a limited set of redirecting objects, such as ramps, bumpers, or portals, within the level layout. Once positioned, the player taps a cannon to fire a fixed number of balls, typically 15-25, which then fall under gravity and interact with the placed objects. The primary objective is to guide all launched balls into 3-5 designated target buckets, with a level failing if the ball count in any required bucket is insufficient. Successfully completing a level unlocks the next in a linear sequence, and reaching specific level milestones (e.g., every 10 levels) unlocks new cosmetic cannon designs, increasing visual variety. Difficulty scales by introducing more intricate obstacle arrangements, increasing the number of required buckets, and demanding higher precision in object placement.
game_info.description: Guide cannon balls into target buckets by strategically placing ramps and bumpers! Each level gives you a limited number of redirecting objects to position before firing 20 balls from the cannon. Your goal is to get the required number of balls into each colored bucket to complete the level. Plan carefully - once you fire, physics takes over! Complete levels to unlock new cannon designs and face increasingly challenging puzzles.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move selected object (when in placement mode)
Space: Fire cannon (when ready) / Reset level (after firing)
Z: Rotate selected object counterclockwise
X: Rotate selected object clockwise
A/D: Cycle through available objects to place
Enter: Start game from title screen
Esc: Pause/Unpause game
R: Return to start screen

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic object placement and cannon firing mechanics. Verifies that players can select objects, move them around the playfield, rotate them, and successfully fire the cannon. Also tests that balls spawn correctly and interact with placed objects through physics.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Automatically places a ramp in the center of the screen at a 45-degree angle, positions a bumper near a bucket, then fires the cannon immediately. Monitors ball spawning rate and verifies physics interactions with placed objects.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Test succeeds if all 20 balls spawn from the cannon, at least some balls interact with the placed ramp (changing trajectory), and balls eventually come to rest in buckets or on the ground. No crashes or physics errors should occur.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing win condition by strategically guiding balls into buckets to meet level requirements. This validates that the game correctly tracks balls in each bucket and triggers the win state when requirements are satisfied.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Places ramps and bumpers in optimal positions to funnel balls directly into the required buckets for level 1. Uses precise angles and positioning to maximize ball collection. Fires cannon and waits for all balls to settle before checking win condition.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Test succeeds when the level completion screen appears showing "LEVEL COMPLETE!" after the required number of balls land in each target bucket. The game should transition to the next level setup, and no balls should be left bouncing indefinitely.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing object rotation mechanics and placement constraints. Verifies that objects can be rotated to various angles and that placement respects boundaries, preventing objects from being positioned outside the playable area or overlapping with static level elements.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Test succeeds if all objects rotate smoothly without visual glitches, objects cannot be placed outside the canvas bounds or overlapping with cannon/buckets, and rotation angles wrap correctly. Objects should visually indicate their current rotation state.
TEST_3.description: Testing object rotation mechanics and placement constraints. Verifies that objects can be rotated to various angles and that placement respects boundaries, preventing objects from being positioned outside the playable area or overlapping with static level elements.
TEST_3.expected: Test succeeds if all objects rotate smoothly without visual glitches, objects cannot be placed outside the canvas bounds or overlapping with cannon/buckets, and rotation angles wrap correctly. Objects should visually indicate their current rotation state.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing level progression and difficulty scaling. Validates that completing a level unlocks the next one, that level difficulty increases appropriately, and that level state is maintained correctly throughout the game session.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Test succeeds if each completed level transitions smoothly to the next, level numbers increment correctly, difficulty increases measurably (more buckets, higher requirements, or fewer objects), and game state remains consistent without memory leaks or broken references.
TEST_4.description: Testing level progression and difficulty scaling. Validates that completing a level unlocks the next one, that level difficulty increases appropriately, and that level state is maintained correctly throughout the game session.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing pause and restart functionality during active gameplay. Ensures that pausing stops all physics simulation, the game can be resumed correctly, and restarting returns to proper state without lingering objects or incorrect ball counts.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Test succeeds if pausing immediately freezes all ball movement and physics, resuming continues from exact same state, and restarting cleanly removes all balls and resets placement phase. No duplicate objects or phantom balls should remain after restart.
TEST_5.description: Testing pause and restart functionality during active gameplay. Ensures that pausing stops all physics simulation, the game can be resumed correctly, and restarting returns to proper state without lingering objects or incorrect ball counts.
TEST_5.expected: Test succeeds if pausing immediately freezes all ball movement and physics, resuming continues from exact same state, and restarting cleanly removes all balls and resets placement phase. No duplicate objects or phantom balls should remain after restart.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing bucket detection and ball counting accuracy. Validates that the game correctly identifies when balls land in buckets, maintains accurate counts for each bucket, and doesn't miscount balls that bounce out or miss entirely.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Test succeeds if bucket counters increment only when balls are genuinely inside bucket boundaries, balls that bounce out aren't counted, the total count across all buckets plus missed balls equals 20, and counts remain stable after all motion stops.
TEST_6.description: Testing bucket detection and ball counting accuracy. Validates that the game correctly identifies when balls land in buckets, maintains accurate counts for each bucket, and doesn't miscount balls that bounce out or miss entirely.
TEST_6.expected: Test succeeds if bucket counters increment only when balls are genuinely inside bucket boundaries, balls that bounce out aren't counted, the total count across all buckets plus missed balls equals 20, and counts remain stable after all motion stops.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing cosmetic cannon unlock system at level milestones. Verifies that reaching specific level numbers (e.g., level 10) unlocks new cannon visual designs and that these cosmetics persist and can be viewed or selected by the player.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Test succeeds if new cannon designs unlock at correct level milestones, unlock notification appears briefly, cannon visual changes are applied and visible in subsequent levels, and unlocked designs are saved in game state without requiring replay of earlier levels.
TEST_7.description: Testing cosmetic cannon unlock system at level milestones. Verifies that reaching specific level numbers (e.g., level 10) unlocks new cannon visual designs and that these cosmetics persist and can be viewed or selected by the player.
TEST_7.expected: Test succeeds if new cannon designs unlock at correct level milestones, unlock notification appears briefly, cannon visual changes are applied and visible in subsequent levels, and unlocked designs are saved in game state without requiring replay of earlier levels.
